messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: Thanks. I haven’t heard of the AltaVox and will kin into it. I have put up a seedstudio one and it seems fine. I’ve seen sone talk about one watt repeaters (or nearly so) and have wondered if it it’s important. Height and antenna do seem more important, I agree.

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: Height and line of sight are far more important. 1W boards are more a case of "BIGGAR NUMBAH, MOAR BETTARH... ????". If the other end isn't also using a high power radio, you end up with one way communication, which isn't very helpful. And as the mesh fills in and you get nodes closer together, you don't really need the higher power any more. The biggest use for the high-power radios is if you have a long feed line between the radio and the antenna. But it's much better to just design things so the radio and the antenna are in close proximity to each other. (edited)
messages.content_html: Height and line of sight are far more important. 1W boards are more a case of "BIGGAR NUMBAH, MOAR BETTARH... ????". If the other end isn't also using a high power radio, you end up with one way communication, which isn't very helpful. And as the mesh fills in and you get nodes closer together, you don't really need the higher power any more. The biggest use for the high-power radios is if you have a long feed line between the radio and the antenna. But it's much better to just design things so the radio and the antenna are in close proximity to each other. (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 2

messages.author: Hugo
messages.text: Cascadia Mesh has some DIY repeater builds at https://cascadiamesh.org/repeater-builds/ , with some showing the type of range they get. There is info on some 1W builds there, though I think generally when people are talking about 1W builds recently that's mostly been the WisMesh 1W kit (e.g. https://store.rakwireless.com/collections/meshtastic-1w-boosters) or Heltec v4.3 (ref: https://discord.com/channels/1343693475589263471/1391673743453192242/1487849272639623218) But, e.g. on the Cascadia Mesh, Jade has examples of the Jade Node hitting ~100 miles / 160km with a basic ANT-916 with good height and LoS, and that's using the same nRF52840 and SX1262 as you get in the SenseCap Solar P1.

messages.author: Hugo
messages.text: Yea, I'd seen similar conversations around 1W and folks offering advice that sure it can be helpful if you're aiming for longer backhaul and can ensure you've got 1W on each end, but less useful for "general coverage + inter-repeater" usage.
